title: NOC 42203 – Instructors of Persons with Disabilities | GTR Immigration
description: Instructors of Persons with Disabilities under NOC 42203 are specialized professionals who support individuals with physical, intellectual, visual, hearing, or multiple disabilities to develop independence, life skills, communication abilities, and vocational readiness. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation requires formal training in special education or rehabilitation-related disciplines. These professionals work in schools, rehabilitation centers, community agencies, and specialized institutions, helping clients achieve greater autonomy and social integration.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group422 – Paraprofessional occupations in legal, social, community, and education servicesMinor Group4220 – Paraprofessional occupationsNOC Code42203

common_job_titles: Instructor of persons with mobility impairment
Instructor of persons who are deaf
Instructor of persons who are hard of hearing
Instructor of persons with learning disability
Instructor of persons with visual impairment
Instructor of persons with special needs
Teacher for persons with intellectual disabilities
duties: Develop individualized education and intervention plans
Assess physical, cognitive, emotional, and mobility limitations
Assist individuals in developing life skills and vocational skills
Train clients in the use of assistive devices and prosthetics
Teach Braille and adaptive reading techniques
Provide mobility training using canes or guide systems
Teach lip-reading, sign language, and communication techniques
Support speech development for hearing-impaired individuals
Collaborate with rehabilitation counsellors, speech-language pathologists, and occupational therapists
Provide guidance to families on rehabilitation techniques
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in:Special educationRehabilitationOrientation and mobilityVisual or hearing impairmentIntellectual disability support
A bachelor’s degree in special education, social work, psychology, or related field may be required
Practical training and experience working with persons with disabilities are highly valued.
salary_prospects: Instructors of Persons with Disabilities typically earn between:
$34,700 – $79,600 per year,depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Ontario and larger provinces tend to offer higher median wages.
labour_market_demand: Instructors of Persons with Disabilities are employed in:
Increased awareness of disability support services
Opportunities in education and healthcare sectors
Stable employment in public and non-profit sectors
Opportunities for specialization (visual impairment, mobility training, hearing support)
Career growth into supervisory or specialist roles
Inclusion and accessibility initiatives across Canada continue to support employment growth in this field.
pathways: Instructors of Persons with Disabilities fall under TEER 2 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
